title: -Allocation Request: MTC Safe Routes to Transit and Bay Trail Program: Adeline Street Quick Build

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the Public Works Department to submit a grant funding
allocation request to the Metropolitan Transportation Commission (MTC) for the Adeline
Street Quick-Build project.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The estimated total cost of the Adeline Street Quick-Build Project (Project) is
$1,041,000. To access $922,000 in funding from the Regional Measure 3: 2025 Safe
Routes to Transit and Bay Trail Program (SR2TBT), the proposed allocation request
must be adopted by Council. This Metropolitan Transportation Commission (MTC)
program requires that the City provide a minimum 11.47% of the total funding in
matching funds. The City will provide a $119,000 match to meet MTC’s 11.47%
requirement, available in fiscal year 2027 from Measure BB Fund 134 (Bicycle and
Pedestrian).
sections.current_situation:
On October 1, 2024, Council adopted Resolution No. 71,523-N.S., authorizing the filing
of an application for funding for the Adeline Quick Build to the MTC’s Safe Routes to
Transit and Bay Trail program. On February 26, 2025, MTC recommended funding the
project for the full $922,000 requested.
The Council must adopt a resolution authorizing the allocation request to be submitted
to MTC to be able to access these funds and begin the project. MTC’s Resolution No.
4404 Regional Measure 3 Policies and Procedures requires that project sponsors
submit a Resolution adopted by the City Council supporting the allocation request. The
allocation process for Regional Measure 3 capital projects is required for executing
funding agreements.
This project addresses bicyclist and pedestrian safety concerns in one of MTC’s Equity
Priority Communities (EPC) by combining recommendations from several Council
approved plans. The EPC designation helps provide funding and assistance to census

The safety countermeasures and traffic calming improvements in the Project were
identified in the following Council approved plans:
 2017 Berkeley Bike Plan,
 2020 Vision Zero Plan,
 2020 Berkeley Pedestrian Plan, and
 the 2020 Adeline Corridor Specific Plan.

sections.background:
The Adeline Street Quick Build project will complement planned improvements on the
City of Oakland’s side of the border. The City of Oakland is currently developing the
Martin Luther King Jr. Way Complete Streets and Paving Project, which includes
proposed pedestrian, bicycle, and transit improvements that will ultimately connect to
the City of Berkeley’s Adeline Street Quick Build facilities.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Increasing cycling and walking in Berkeley will help the City achieve the Berkeley
Climate Action Plan greenhouse gas emission reduction targets of 80% below year
2000 levels by 2050. The project is exempt from the California Environmental Quality
Act under Public Resources Code section 21080.25(b)(1) because it consists of
pedestrian and bicycle facilities that improve safety, access, or mobility within the public
right-of-way. The project does not induce single-occupancy vehicle trips, add additional

highway lanes, widen highways, or add physical infrastructure or striping to highways
except for minor modifications needed for the efficient and safe movement of transit
vehicles, bicycles, or high-occupancy vehicles. The project will not include the addition
of any auxiliary lanes and will not require the demolition of affordable housing units.
Pursuant to Public Resources Code section 21080.25(f), Council certifies that the
project will be completed by a skilled and trained workforce.
sections.rationale:
This project implements planned improvements that have already been approved by
Council in the following plans:
 2017 Berkeley Bike Plan,
 2020 Vision Zero Plan,
 2020 Berkeley Pedestrian Plan, and the
 2020 Adeline Corridor Specific Plan
The grant application for the project was previously adopted by Council Resolution No.
71,523-N.S. Adoption of this Resolution ensures the project can be implemented within
the planned schedule, bringing improvements to a high injury corridor. Additionally,
these MTC funds are vitally important to ensuring that the City has adequate budget to
move forward with planned improvements, despite anticipated future fiscal year deficits.
sections.alternatives:
Not submitting this grant funding allocation request to MTC is an alternative that would
leave the allocated funding for the City unclaimed. The City would be financially
responsible for the full cost of all improvements to this corridor. This option was deemed
inadvisable, as it would directly delay the implementation of critical bicyclist and
pedestrian safety measures along Adeline Street—a high-injury corridor identified in the
2020 Vision Zero Plan. Additionally, it would result in forfeiture of $922,000 in Regional
Measure 3 funding, thereby missing a significant opportunity to advance Council-
approved transportation safety and climate action goals.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, Public Works, 510-981-6396
Mark Helmbrecht, Transportation Manager, Public Works, 510-981-6403
Babak Dorji, Senior Planner, Public Works, 510-981-7073

WHEREAS, SB 595 (Chapter 650, Statutes 2017), commonly referred as Regional
Measure 3, identified projects eligible to receive funding under the Regional Measure 3

WHEREAS, the Metropolitan Transportation Commission (MTC) is responsible for
funding projects eligible for Regional Measure 3 funds, pursuant to Streets and Highways
Code Section 30914.7(a) and (c); and
WHEREAS, MTC has established a process whereby eligible transportation project
sponsors may submit allocation requests for Regional Measure 3 funding; and
WHEREAS, allocation requests to MTC must be submitted consistent with procedures
and conditions as outlined in Regional Measure 3 Policies and Procedures (MTC
Resolution No. 4404; and
WHEREAS, Metropolitan Transportation Commission is the sponsor of Project 4.3
Adeline Street Quick Build in the Regional Measure 3 Expenditure Plan; and
WHEREAS, the 4.3 Adeline Street Quick Build is eligible for consideration in the Regional
Measure 3 Expenditure Plan, as identified in California Streets and Highways Code
Section 30914.7(a); and
WHEREAS, Metropolitan Transportation Commission designated City of Berkeley as an
entity that is eligible to request Regional Measure 3 funds for the 4.3 Adeline Street Quick
Build project; and
WHEREAS, the Regional Measure 3 updated Initial Project Report (IPR) and allocation
request, attached hereto and incorporated herein as though set forth at length, lists the
project, purpose, schedule, budget, expenditure and cash flow plan for
